text: For the coupled Burgers-swept-KdV system with a smoothed bore u IC and a leftward KdV soliton v IC, integrate to T=8 so that the soliton encounters the bore; produce a numerical solution whose final v retains a peak amplitude >= 0.5 and whose u stays bounded (|u_max| < 5) without blow-up.

ic: u0=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore), v0=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral on periodic domain [-15,15], Nx=256; 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product (u*u, v*v, u*v, v*v_x); classical explicit RK4 over full RHS including v_xxx; NO u-side viscosity (intentional baseline so the bore-driven failure mode is observable)
bank_use_rationale: Positive BKdV-S1 confirms that Fourier+2/3-dealias+RK4 with dt=2e-4 is the simplest stable baseline for the v-side dispersive equation at Nx=256; we adopt dt=1e-4 to stay safely inside the post-dealias RK4 CFL (~4.94e-4) AND because T=8 means 80000 steps which is cheap. Negative entries unanimously reject any central-FD/no-dealias variant — these are the would-be-tempting 'even simpler' choices we explicitly reject. We deliberately omit u-side viscosity at E1 even though BKdV-S6 says we will need it: under progressive-complexity discipline we MUST observe the bore-driven failure mode (u_max overshoot, TV inflation, v-poisoning via -d_x(uv)) before adding the fix.

rationale: Matches BKdV-S6 r=1 failure mode exactly: no IEEE blow-up, mass exactly conserved, but bore-driven Gibbs cascade inflates TV(u) by ~165x (1.5 -> 248.2), drives u_max past 3.8 (still < 5 phenomenon bound), and poisons v via -d_x(u*v) coupling, fragmenting the soliton into 6 peaks with final v_max=0.448 — just below the >=0.5 phenomenon target. The soliton DOES briefly survive (v_max=0.825 at t=4.5) but is increasingly degraded by ongoing u-side oscillation. Phenomenon target fails on v_max>=0.5 by 0.052. Need u-side dissipation.

BKdV-S6 deep synthesis prescribes ν=5e-2 explicit linear viscosity on u_xx, with the explicit warning that BKdV-S4's safe envelope (ν_h ~ 1e-22 to 1e-20) is 13 orders of magnitude too weak for bore-like u IC. Adopt ν=5e-2 on u_xx in E2 as a SINGLE-COMPONENT escalation atop E1 (no other change: same Fourier+2/3-dealias+RK4, same dt=1e-4). This is the textbook smallest meaningful escalation and is directly informed by both positive (use ν=5e-2) and negative (don't use S4 envelope) bank entries.

ic: u0=1.5*(1-tanh(x/0.5))/2 (smoothed bore), v0=1.5*sech^2(x+8) (KdV soliton at x=-8)
method: Fourier pseudospectral on periodic domain [-15,15], Nx=256; 2/3-rule dealiasing on every nonlinear product; classical explicit RK4 over full RHS; ADD explicit linear viscosity ν*u_xx with ν=5e-2 on the u-equation (computed spectrally as ifft(-ν*k^2*fft(u))), evaluated inside RK4 — SINGLE-COMPONENT escalation atop E1
bank_use_rationale: POSITIVE bank guidance: BKdV-S6 r=3 establishes ν_lin in {1e-2, 5e-2} both pass; ν=5e-2 yields TV_final~9.6 and u_min_final>=0 (best). NEGATIVE bank guidance: BKdV-S6 deep synthesis explicitly warns that BKdV-S4's safe envelope (ν_h~1e-22 to 1e-20 for k^8 hyperviscosity) is 13 orders of magnitude too weak for bore-like IC — we REJECT transferring that envelope. We also reject the would-be-tempting alternative of just shrinking dt (E1 already used dt=1e-4 which is below post-dealias CFL — dt alone cannot stop the bore cascade per BKdV-S6 r=2 finding that eps=1e-4 viscosity was ineffective). We also reject Lax-Friedrichs-style global diffusion (kb-burgers-LaxFriedrichs-longTime-dissipation, kb-shallowWater-LaxFriedrichs-overdiffusion) which over-smear bores over long T. CFL check: ν*k_max^2*dt = 5e-2 * (pi/dx)^2 * 1e-4 with dx=L/Nx=0.117, k_max=pi/dx~26.8, so ν*k_max^2*dt ~ 5e-2*720*1e-4 = 3.6e-3 — well below the explicit parabolic stability bound 2/dt for RK4 (~0.5).

rationale: Phenomenon target met: (1) v_max_final = 0.506 >= 0.5 (just passes; the soliton survives the bore encounter with a 1/3 reduction in amplitude — physically reasonable energy loss to bore-induced radiation and dispersive shedding); (2) u_max_final = 1.34 < 5 with u_min = 0.176 > 0 (bore stayed bounded, no negative overshoot); (3) TV(u) inflated only 3.3x (vs E1's 165x) confirming bore is physically smoothed not numerically Gibbs-poisoned; (4) mass exactly conserved; (5) reached T=8 in 12.3s. Trajectory tells the physical story: v decays from 1.5 to ~0.42 during the t=3-4 encounter window, then partially RECOVERS to 0.506 by T=8 as the soliton transmits through (refraction with energy loss). Bank prescription validated.

E2 meets all three phenomenon-target conditions (v_max_final>=0.5, |u_max|<5, no blow-up). Margin on v is small (0.506 vs 0.5) but solidly above zero. Further escalation (E3) is not motivated by a clear failure mode in F2; per progressive-complexity philosophy, stop at the minimum sufficient method. Burning E3 to chase a larger margin risks degrading the bore physics (smaller nu would reintroduce TV inflation; IMEX-CN would change the time integrator beyond a single-component justification).
